creator: web@3dresearch.com
text: Some of my computers, which aren't the fastest, have 1,000+ ports, and update them only when 'pkg audit' prompts me. Then I update the basics (pkg(8), perl(1), etc.) and the vulnerable ports + dependencies. Then I 'pkg check -d -a' and update as needed - and this procedure has worked well enough and less time than 'portupgrade -a'. Clearly though, 'portupgrade -a' would have obviated reporting this non-bug; thank you for the reminder!
